docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get from zero to first passing check in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install small-expectations

docs.title: 2. Create config
docs.text:
Fast path:

smallex init --engine sqlite

Or create smallex.toml manually:

Create smallex.toml:

[database]
engine = "sqlite"

[database.connection]
database = "example.db"

docs.title: 3. Add a SQL check
docs.text:
Create tests/01_no_null_emails.sql:

SELECT *
FROM users
WHERE email IS NULL;

Rule of thumb:

  • returns 0 rows => pass
  • returns >= 1 row => fail

docs.title: 4. Run
docs.text:
smallex run

You will see a pytest-style report with:

  • test session header
  • per-test progress (. for pass, F for fail)
  • optional failure details
  • final summary and proper exit code

docs.title: 5. Next steps
docs.text:
• For production usage patterns, go to Advanced Guide.
  • For backend connection settings, go to Connection Specs.
  • For all CLI options, go to CLI Reference.

docs.title: Advanced Guide
docs.text: This page covers features you will use in team workflows and CI.

docs.title: Recommended project layout
docs.text:
.
├── smallex.toml
└── tests/
    ├── 01_no_null_emails.sql
    └── 02_business_rules.sql

Run from repository root:

smallex run

docs.title: Multiple tests inside one SQL file
docs.text:
You can declare multiple logical checks in a single file using metadata comments.

-- smallex:test: no_null_emails
-- smallex:message: users.email should never be null
SELECT id, email
FROM users
WHERE email IS NULL;

-- smallex:test: no_duplicate_emails
-- smallex:message: users.email should be unique
SELECT email, COUNT(*) AS duplicates
FROM users
WHERE email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY email
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

Behavior:

  • -- smallex:test: starts a new logical test block
  • -- smallex:message: adds custom failure text to that test
  • if no test marker exists, the whole file becomes one test

docs.title: Useful command variants
docs.text:
Use custom config path:

smallex run --config config/smallex.prod.toml

Use custom tests directory:

smallex run --tests-dir expectations

Control color output:

smallex run --color auto
smallex run --color yes
smallex run --color no

docs.title: Failure rows output (debugging failed checks)
docs.text:
By default, failing rows are not printed/exported.

Show top failing rows in terminal:

smallex run \
  --failure-rows-mode terminal \
  --failure-rows-limit 5

Export failing rows to CSV files:

smallex run \
  --failure-rows-mode csv \
  --failure-rows-csv-limit 10000 \
  --failure-rows-dir .smallex/failures

Do both:

smallex run \
  --failure-rows-mode both \
  --failure-rows-limit 5 \
  --failure-rows-csv-limit 10000

CSV naming pattern:

  • <sql_file_stem>__<test_name>.csv

docs.title: Exit codes for automation
docs.text:
• 0: all checks passed
  • 1: one or more checks failed
  • 2: configuration/runtime error

This allows straightforward CI integration.

docs.title: CI example
docs.text:
smallex run --config smallex.toml --tests-dir tests

Fail your pipeline when exit code is non-zero.

docs.title: Common rollout practices for data teams
docs.text:
• Keep checks near the data project code (tests/ or expectations/).
  • Start with high-signal rules (nulls, uniqueness, referential integrity).
  • Use custom --smallex:message values as runbook hints for fast triage.
  • Use CSV export mode in CI to preserve evidence for failed checks.

docs.title: Main command
docs.text:
smallex run [options]

Other commands:

  • smallex init: scaffold starter config + sample SQL test
  • smallex validate-config: validate config and test database connectivity

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• --config: path to TOML config file (default: smallex.toml)
  • --tests-dir: root directory to discover .sql tests recursively (default: tests)
  • --env: named database connection environment from [database.connections.<name>]
  • --color: auto | yes | no (default: auto)
  • --failure-rows-mode: none | terminal | csv | both (default: none)
  • --failure-rows-limit: terminal rows per failing test (default: 5)
  • --failure-rows-csv-limit: max CSV rows per failing test (default: 10000)
  • --failure-rows-dir: CSV output directory (default: .smallex/failures)

docs.title: What the run does
docs.text:
1. Loads TOML config.
  2. Discovers .sql files recursively under --tests-dir.
  3. Parses one or more logical tests per file.
  4. Executes each test query.
  5. Marks pass when query returns zero rows; fail otherwise.
  6. Prints pytest-style summary output.

docs.title: Exit codes
docs.text:
• 0: all tests passed
  • 1: at least one test failed
  • 2: invalid config, invalid CLI args, or runtime error

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Basic run:

smallex run

Run from non-default config and tests location:

smallex run --config conf/smallex.toml --tests-dir expectations

Run with a specific connection environment:

smallex run --env dev

Initialize a new project:

smallex init --engine sqlite

Validate configuration and connection:

smallex validate-config --config smallex.toml --env dev

Show failing rows in terminal:

smallex run --failure-rows-mode terminal --failure-rows-limit 10

Export failing rows to CSV:

smallex run --failure-rows-mode csv --failure-rows-dir artifacts/smallex

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: The CLI reads TOML config from smallex.toml by default.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
[database]
engine = "sqlite" # sqlite | snowflake | databricks
default_connection = "dev" # optional

[database.connection]
# backend-specific connector args

[database.connections.dev]
# backend-specific connector args

[database.connections.prod]
# backend-specific connector args

docs.title: database.engine
docs.text:
Allowed values:

  • sqlite
  • snowflake
  • databricks

docs.title: database.connection
docs.text:
A table containing connector arguments. Values are passed directly to the selected connector after required field validation.

See Connection Specs for backend-by-backend required and optional fields.

docs.title: database.connections
docs.text:
Use this table to define multiple named connection environments in a single file:

  • [database.connections.<name>] for each environment (dev, development, prod, etc.)
  • optional [database].default_connection to select a default when --env is not provided

Selection behavior:

  • If smallex run --env <name> is used, that named connection is selected.
  • If --env is omitted and default_connection exists, that connection is selected.
  • If --env is omitted and default_connection is missing, default is used when present.
  • If exactly one named connection exists, it is used automatically.
  • If multiple named connections exist without default and without --env, the run fails with a config error.

docs.title: Legacy compatibility
docs.text:
For backward compatibility, if [database.connection] is missing, keys in [database] (except engine and module) are treated as connection options.

Legacy-style SQLite example:

[database]
engine = "sqlite"
database = "example.db"

docs.title: File location and CLI override
docs.text:
Default file path:

  • smallex.toml

Override path with:

smallex run --config path/to/smallex.toml

docs.title: Connection Specs
docs.text:
small-expectations supports sqlite, snowflake, and databricks.

Connection values are read from:

[database]
engine = "..."

[database.connection]
# backend-specific connector args

All keys under [database.connection] are passed directly to the selected Python connector.

docs.title: SQLite
docs.text:
Connector module: sqlite3.connect(...)

Required keys:

  • database: path to .db file (or :memory:)

Minimal example:

[database]
engine = "sqlite"

[database.connection]
database = "example.db"

Common optional keys (passed through):

  • timeout
  • detect_types
  • isolation_level
  • check_same_thread
  • uri

URI example:

[database]
engine = "sqlite"

[database.connection]
database = "file:example.db?mode=ro"
uri = true

docs.title: Snowflake
docs.text:
Connector module: snowflake.connector.connect(...)

Required keys validated by small-expectations:

  • account
  • user
  • password
  • warehouse
  • database
  • schema

Minimal example:

[database]
engine = "snowflake"

[database.connection]
account = "xy12345.eu-west-1"
user = "analytics_user"
password = "your-password"
warehouse = "COMPUTE_WH"
database = "ANALYTICS"
schema = "PUBLIC"

Common optional keys (passed through):

  • role
  • authenticator
  • session_parameters
  • client_session_keep_alive
  • application

docs.title: Databricks
docs.text:
Connector module: databricks.sql.connect(...)

Required keys validated by small-expectations:

  • server_hostname
  • http_path
  • access_token

Minimal example:

[database]
engine = "databricks"

[database.connection]
server_hostname = "adb-1234567890123456.7.azuredatabricks.net"
http_path = "/sql/1.0/warehouses/abc123"
access_token = "dapi..."

Common optional keys (passed through):

  • catalog
  • schema
  • session_configuration
  • http_headers

Note: small-expectations does not expand environment variables inside TOML values by itself. If you need secret injection, generate the TOML file before running or use your CI/CD secret templating flow.

docs.title: Validation errors you may see
docs.text:
Missing required fields are validated before connecting.

Example:

Backend 'snowflake' is missing required connection fields: schema

Unknown engine example:

Unsupported database engine 'postgres'. Supported: databricks, snowflake, sqlite

docs.title: smallex.backends
docs.text: Backend registry and factory helpers.

docs.title: get_backend(engine: str) -> BaseDatabaseBackend
docs.text:
Create a backend instance from an engine identifier.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default engine str

Backend identifier from config.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description BaseDatabaseBackend BaseDatabaseBackend

A concrete backend instance.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the engine is unknown.

docs.title: smallex.backends.base
docs.text: Base interfaces and shared utilities for database backends.

docs.title: BaseDatabaseBackend
docs.text:
Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for all supported database backends.

Concrete backends define connector import details and required options. They then reuse :meth:connect to validate config and open a connection.

Attributes:

Name Type Description engine_name str

Public engine identifier used in config files.

module_path str

Import path for the connector module.

connect_attr str

Connector function name in the imported module.

required_connection_fields tuple[str, ...]

Required config keys for the backend.

docs.title: connect(options: Mapping[str, object]) -> ConnectionProtocol
docs.text:
Open a database connection using the backend connector.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default options Mapping[str, object]

Connection keyword arguments passed to the connector.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description ConnectionProtocol ConnectionProtocol

A DB-API-like connection object.

docs.title: prepare_connection_options(options: Mapping[str, object]) -> dict[str, object]
docs.text:
Transform user config into connector-ready options.

Subclasses can override this to map normalized values to connector-specific keys and to remove framework-level keys that should not be passed to the underlying connector.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default options Mapping[str, object]

Raw connection mapping from config.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, object]

dict[str, object]: Connector-ready connection options.

docs.title: test_connection(connection: ConnectionProtocol, options: Mapping[str, object]) -> None
docs.text: Verify a backend connection is usable.

docs.title: validate_connection_options(options: Mapping[str, object]) -> None
docs.text:
Validate connection options before opening a connection.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default options Mapping[str, object]

Connection keyword arguments from user config.

required

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If one or more required fields are missing.

docs.title: DatabaseConfig dataclass
docs.text:
Configuration payload used to build a backend connection.

Attributes:

Name Type Description engine str

Logical backend name (for example: sqlite or snowflake).

connection dict[str, object]

Keyword arguments passed to the backend connector.

Bases: BaseDatabaseBackend

Backend for local SQLite databases.

Backend for Snowflake using snowflake-connector-python.

Backend for Databricks SQL using databricks-sql-connector.

Expected connection options for token auth (default): - server_hostname - http_path - access_token

docs.text: Supported values for the --color option.

docs.text: Build the top-level command parser.

docs.title: main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int
docs.text: CLI entrypoint used by both console script and tests.

docs.title: smallex.runner
docs.text: Core execution pipeline for SQL expectation tests.

docs.title: FailureRowsConfig dataclass
docs.text:
Configuration controlling how failing rows are collected and reported.

Attributes:

Name Type Description mode str

One of none, terminal, csv, or both.

terminal_limit int

Number of rows to display per failing test in terminal.

csv_limit int

Maximum number of rows to persist per failing test in CSV.

csv_dir Path

Target directory for CSV outputs.

docs.title: csv_enabled() -> bool
docs.text: Return whether CSV failure exports should be produced.

docs.title: terminal_enabled() -> bool
docs.text: Return whether terminal row previews should be produced.

docs.title: FailureRowsMode
docs.text: Supported modes for failure row reporting.

docs.title: TestResult dataclass
docs.text:
Outcome for one SQL expectation.

Attributes:

Name Type Description case SQLTestCase

Source SQL test case metadata.

passed bool

True when query returns zero rows.

row_count int

Number of rows fetched for this failure context.

has_more_rows bool

True when rows exist beyond configured fetch limits.

columns list[str]

Result column names for failing query rows.

sample_rows list[tuple[object, ...]]

Sample rows for terminal reporting.

csv_path Path | None

Generated CSV path when export is enabled.

docs.title: message: str | None property
docs.text: Return user-authored message associated with this test case.

docs.title: node_id: str property
docs.text: Return pytest-like node id for terminal reporting.

docs.title: path: Path property
docs.text: Return source path for compatibility with existing callers.

docs.title: discover_sql_cases(tests_dir: Path) -> list[SQLTestCase]
docs.text: Discover SQL files and parse all SQL test cases.

docs.title: discover_sql_tests(tests_dir: Path) -> list[Path]
docs.text: Discover SQL test scripts recursively under a directory.

docs.title: load_config(config_path: Path, env: str | None = None) -> DatabaseConfig
docs.text: Load and validate CLI config from TOML file.

docs.title: run_all(config_path: Path, tests_dir: Path, *, env: str | None = None, failure_rows: FailureRowsConfig | None = None) -> tuple[list[TestResult], int]
docs.text: Run all SQL expectations and return results plus failed count.

docs.title: run_sql_case(connection: ConnectionProtocol, case: SQLTestCase, *, failure_rows: FailureRowsConfig) -> TestResult
docs.text: Execute one SQL test case and classify pass/fail with diagnostics.

docs.title: smallex.sqltests
docs.text: SQL test file parsing utilities.

docs.title: SQLTestCase dataclass
docs.text:
A single executable SQL expectation parsed from test files.

Attributes:

Name Type Description path Path

Source SQL file path.

name str

Human-readable test name.

message str | None

Optional failure message authored by the developer.

query str

SQL query text to execute.

docs.title: node_id: str property
docs.text: Return pytest-like node id for terminal reporting.

docs.title: parse_sql_file(path: Path) -> list[SQLTestCase]
docs.text:
Parse one SQL file into one or more test cases.

Supported metadata markers

-- smallex:test: <name> -- smallex:message: <message>

Marker semantics
  • test starts a new logical test block when encountered after SQL.
  • message attaches to the next finalized test block.
  • files without markers still produce one test case using full contents.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path Path

SQL file to parse.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[SQLTestCase]

list[SQLTestCase]: Parsed SQL test cases in file order.

docs.title: parse_sql_files(paths: Iterable[Path]) -> list[SQLTestCase]
docs.text: Parse multiple SQL files into a flat list of SQL test cases.
